column.name: effective_date
column.remarks: Date the code becomes effective

column.name: expiry_date
column.remarks: Date the code expires

column.name: update_timestamp
column.remarks: The date and time the value was last modified.

table.name: request_item
table.remarks: A particular item within a request for which services are being requested.

column.name: request_skey
column.remarks: A surrogate key to uniquely identify each Spar Request.

column.name: item_id
column.remarks: A unique identifier for each item within a request. It is alphabetically assigned (A, B,...Z).

column.name: standard_workplan_skey
column.remarks: A surrogate key which uniquely identifies a standard workplan.

column.name: vegetation_code
column.remarks: A code which represents a species of tree or brush.

column.name: sample_id
column.remarks: The identifier of a sample if it is not a seedlot.

column.name: family_lot_number
column.remarks: The unique alphanumeric (key) assigned to a quantity of seed or cones of a particular species and quality from a given location collected at a given time.

column.name: rqst_sdlngs_rsrvd
column.remarks: The number, in thousands, of seedlings that were requested from an owner"s Reserve portion of a lot.

column.name: rqst_sdlngs_srpls
column.remarks: The number, in thousands, of seedlings that were requested from an owner"s Surplus portion of a lot.

column.name: cone_ind
column.remarks: An indicator which represents whether a sample is for cone ("Y" yes) or not ("N" no).

column.name: no_of_containers
column.remarks: The number of containers (sacks of cones/bags of seed) that are part of the request item.

column.name: no_of_cones
column.remarks: The number of cones that are part of the request item if it is a sample.

column.name: cone_volume
column.remarks: The total volume of cones, in hectolitres, that are part of the request item.

column.name: seed_quantity
column.remarks: The total volume of seed, in grams, that are part of the request item.

column.name: sample_elevation
column.remarks: The representative elevation in meters where the sample originated.

column.name: shipto_cli_number
column.remarks: A sequentially assigned number which uniquely identifies a Ministry client.

column.name: shipto_cli_locn_cd
column.remarks: A code to uniquely identify, within each client, the addresses of different divisions or locations at which the client operates. The location code is sequentially assigned starting with "00" for the client"s permanent address.

column.name: required_by_date
column.remarks: The date (year, month and day) on which the requestor would like the services completed.

column.name: latest_actioned_date
column.remarks: The latest date (year, month and day) for which any changes can be applied to the requested item before it is scheduled to be actioned.

column.name: rcmnded_sowing_date
column.remarks: The system"s recommended date in the given sowing year for which sowing should take place for a Seedling Request.

column.name: spcfied_sowing_date
column.remarks: The date in the given sowing year which is specified by the assigned nursery for which sowing should take place for a Seedling Request. This date will override the recommended sowing day.

column.name: tender_number
column.remarks: A number assigned by Silviculture Branch which indicates the tender on which the request item has been assigned.

column.name: tender_bid_group
column.remarks: An identifier within a Tender Number which is assigned by Silviculture Branch which indicates the part of a Tender on which the request item has been assigned.

column.name: tender_issued_date
column.remarks: The date (year, month and day) the request item was assigned to the Tender.

column.name: assigned_price
column.remarks: The assigned price assigned from the tendering process.

column.name: nrsry_obligatn_ind
column.remarks: An indicator which identifies whether or not a nursery assignment has been finalized and obligated.

column.name: nrsry_cli_number
column.remarks: A sequentially assigned number which uniquely identifies a Ministry client.

column.name: nrsry_cli_locn_cd
column.remarks: A code to uniquely identify, within each client, the addresses of different divisions or locations at which the client operates. The location code is sequentially assigned starting with "00" for the client"s permanent address.

column.name: facility_code
column.remarks: A code which represents the type of facility that the seedlings will be grown in.

column.name: extrct_cli_number
column.remarks: A sequentially assigned number which uniquely identifies a Ministry client.

column.name: extrct_cli_locn_cd
column.remarks: A code to uniquely identify, within each client, the addresses of different divisions or locations at which the client operates. The location code is sequentially assigned starting with "00" for the client"s permanent address.

column.name: authorization_code
column.remarks: a code which represents the form by which authority for the request was received.

column.name: srv_blng_create_ts
column.remarks: The time and date when service billing rows were created for the Request Item.

column.name: sample_loc_desc
column.remarks: A free format text description of the location where the sample originated.

column.name: interim_storge_loc
column.remarks: The location where the Request Item will be stored before it reaches the location where the services are to be performed.

column.name: authorized_by_prsn
column.remarks: The name of the person who authorized the request.

column.name: test_approved_userid
column.remarks: The date that the Request Test Services were approved for processing by the Tree Seed Centre.

column.name: test_approved_date
column.remarks: The userid of the user who approved the Request Test Services for processing at the Tree Seed Centre.

column.name: test_update_userid
column.remarks: The date that the Request Test Services were approved for processing by the Tree Seed Centre.

column.name: test_update_timestamp
column.remarks: The date and time that Request Test Services for the Request Item were last updated.

column.name: test_comment
column.remarks: A general comment about the Request Test Services for the Request Item.

column.name: results_audit_action_code
column.remarks: The action code is a code associated with the action. I -- Insert

column.name: description
column.remarks: Full description of code value.

column.name: effective_date
column.remarks: The date the value is available for use.

column.name: expiry_date

table.name: results_audit_detail
table.remarks: Details each of the changes included during an EDT AUDIT EVENT. Shows the value of the modified column before and after the change.

column.name: results_audit_detail_id
column.remarks: The unique identifier for the audit detail entry. Created through a sequence.

column.name: results_audit_event_id
column.remarks: A unique system generated number to use as the key for the table.

column.name: business_identifier
column.remarks: Describes the business key for which data is being audited. Examples of business keys are Licensee ID, Standards Unit ID, Forest Cover Layer ID.

column.name: table_name
column.remarks: The name of the data base table that was modified.

column.name: column_name
column.remarks: The name of the column that the data was modified in.

column.name: old_value
column.remarks: The type of data that was modified. Would be one of, Character, Date or Number. This attribute will be used to determine which of the value fields contains the prior value of the element modified.

column.name: new_value
column.remarks: If the data type was Character, the value of the field before the change.

column.name: entry_userid
column.remarks: The userid of the person who changed the data.

column.name: entry_timestamp
column.remarks: The date and time the data change occurred.

table.name: results_audit_event
table.remarks: Stores Audit Information such as openings submitted, activities submitted, changes in statuses, etc.

column.name: results_audit_event_id
column.remarks: A unique system generated number to use as the key for the table.

column.name: opening_id
column.remarks: System generated value uniquely identifying the opening.

column.name: standards_regime_id
column.remarks: Unique identifier for the STANDARD REGIME entry.

column.name: silviculture_project_id
column.remarks: A system generated key which uniquely identifies an occurrence of a Silviculture Project.

column.name: results_audit_action_code
column.remarks: The action code is a code associated with the action. I -- Insert

column.name: action_date
column.remarks: The date the action occurred that is being logged.

column.name: description
column.remarks: Description of the action that occurred. IE: Opening ID 8736636 submitted, or Opening status changed from SUB to APP.

column.name: user_id
column.remarks: The user id were the email was sent.

column.name: email_sent_ind
column.remarks: Indicator used to indicate if email was sent or not for this audit event.

column.name: xml_submission_id
column.remarks: The submission file ID that was given when the data was loaded.

column.name: opening_amendment_number

The FIP based version of Damage Agent code, retained on an as is basis until migration to a stable Ministry version of Damage Agent is availible.

table.name: silv_relief_application
table.remarks: Application for relief from obligation or montary reembursement for damage done by natural disasters etc.

column.name: silv_relief_application_id
column.remarks: Uniquely identifies an Application for Relief from obligations.

column.name: silv_relief_application_no
column.remarks: The sequetial number given to the relief application baed on the number of application submitted for the opening only.

column.name: activity_treatment_unit_id
column.remarks: System generated value uniquely identifying the Activity Treatment Unit.

column.name: silv_relief_appl_status_code
column.remarks: Code identifying the status of the relief application.

column.name: pre_event_forest_cover_date
column.remarks: Most recent observation date describing the forest condition prior to the event.

column.name: post_event_forest_cover_date
column.remarks: Most recent observation date after the damaging event demonstrating the current forest condition leading to the relief request.

column.name: amendment_opening_id
column.remarks: System generated value uniquely identifying the opening.

column.name: opening_amendment_number
column.remarks: A system generated value uniquely identifying an amendment.

column.name: obligation_relief_ind
column.remarks: Denotes whether this application is applying for full relief from obligations.

column.name: obligation_met_ind
column.remarks: Indicates if the Obligation cannot be met without significant extra expense due to the damaging event.

column.name: cause_damage_ind
column.remarks: Indicates if the Obligation holder did not cause or contribute to the cause of the damage.

column.name: officially_induced_error_ind
column.remarks: Indicates if the Obligation holder contributed to the cause of the damage but only as a result of an officially induced error.

column.name: due_diligence_ind
column.remarks: Indicates if the Obligation holder exercised due diligence in relation to the cause of the damage.

column.name: authorization_checked_ind
column.remarks: Indicates if the submitter is authorized by the holder of these silviculture obligations to make this submission and confirms their appropriate professional designation to make this submission.

column.name: submitted_date
column.remarks: The date and time the application was submitted.

column.name: submitted_by_userid
column.remarks: The userid of the individual who submitted the application.

column.name: approved_date
column.remarks: The date and time the application was approved.

column.name: approved_by_userid
column.remarks: The userid of the individual who approved the application.

column.name: reject_comment
column.remarks: If the application is rejected, a reason must be provided.

table.name: silviculture_project
table.remarks: A administrative instrument that allows Ministry staff to plan and execute contracts for silviculture treatments. Collects the general project information recorded during Planting, Site Preparation, Pruning and Survey activities

column.name: silviculture_project_id
column.remarks: A system generated key which uniquely identifies an occurrence of a Silviculture Project.

column.name: silv_base_code
column.remarks: Identifies a primary category of Silviculture Activity.

column.name: fiscal_year
column.remarks: The fiscal year in which the project commenced, or, in the case of a planned project, is expected to commence. A component of the concatenated business key which consists of: SILV BASE CODE FISCAL YEAR ORG UNIT CODE PROJECT SEQUENCE e.g. PL2006DCR035A

column.name: org_unit_no
column.remarks: Unique physical identifier for the storage of organizational unit codes for the Ministry of Forests" offices. Values stored here are for the computer"s use only, and are not to be used by people as "ministry codes".

column.name: project_sequence
column.remarks: A one to four character identifier that is meaningful to the user and assists in distinguishing one Project from another within the same SILV BASE CODE, FISCAL YEAR, ORG UNIT CODE. A component of the concatenated business key which consists of: SILV BASE CODE FISCAL YEAR ORG UNIT CODE PROJECT SEQUENCE e.g. PL2006DCR035A

column.name: project_location
column.remarks: A comment regarding the general location of the project.

column.name: silv_project_status_code
column.remarks: A code to indicate the current status of a SILVICULTURE PROJECT.

column.name: client_number
column.remarks: Sequentially assigned number to identify a ministry client.

column.name: start_date
column.remarks: The date that the project actually commenced, or, in the case of a planned project, the date it is currently scheduled to commence.

column.name: view_date
column.remarks: The planned or actual date of viewing the contract particulars.

column.remarks: The userid of the contract coordinator for the project.

column.remarks: The planned or actual total overhead costs associated with the project.

table.name: stocking_ecology
table.remarks: Primarily a classification of the land base in terms of biogeoclimatic and physical factors.

column.name: stocking_ecology_id
column.remarks: System generated value uniquely identifying the SU Ecology (Eco Strata).

column.name: opening_id
column.remarks: System generated value uniquely identifying the opening.

column.name: stocking_standard_unit_id
column.remarks: System generated value uniquely identifing the stocking standards.

column.name: bec_region_code
column.remarks: A code identifying the biogeoclimatic region.

column.name: bgc_zone_code
column.remarks: A large geographic area with a broadly homogeneous macroclimate which influences the development of vegetation and soil.

column.name: bgc_subzone_code
column.remarks: A code for the bio-geoclimatic sub-zone for the area.

column.name: bgc_variant
column.remarks: A code for the biogeoclimatic variant. A division of the BGC Subzone on the basis of differences in floristic composition of the zonal ecosystem, but usually on the basis of differences in the cover and vigour of the plant species.

column.name: bgc_phase
column.remarks: A code for the biogeoclimatic phase. Accomodates the variation, resulting from local relief, in the regional climate of the subzones and variants.

column.name: bec_site_series
column.remarks: A code for the biogeoclimatic site series. Site series is the consideration of all ecosystems capable of producing vegetation belonging to the same plant association at climax. A code for a site series which is defined as all land areas capable of producing vegetation belonging to the same plant association (or sometimes, subassociation) within a biogeoclimatic subzone or variant. Two-digit numeric codes are usually forested site series; four-character alphnumeric codes are non-forested site series including wetlands, grasslands, talus, rock outcrops, etc. Any two-digit numeric codes for non-forested site series will be migrated to the alphanumeric codes whenever field guides are updated.

column.name: bec_site_type
column.remarks: A code for the biogeoclimatic site type. Site type is the partitionment of the site series according to one or more critical site factors thought to affect ecosystem response to management treatments.

column.name: bec_seral
column.remarks: The seral (often termed successional) classification in BEC is an integration of site and vegetation classifications with structural stage development.

column.name: entry_userid
column.remarks: The USERID of the individual who entered the information.

column.name: entry_timestamp
column.remarks: The date and time the information was entered.

column.name: update_userid
column.remarks: The USERID of the individual who last updated the information.

column.name: update_timestamp
column.remarks: The date and time of the last update.

column.name: revision_count
column.remarks: Internal counter used by the system to avoid conflicting updates to the record.

A code for a site series which is defined as all land areas capable of producing vegetation belonging to the same plant association (or sometimes, subassociation) within a biogeoclimatic subzone or variant. Two-digit numeric codes are usually forested site series; four-character alphnumeric codes are non-forested site series including wetlands, grasslands, talus, rock outcrops, etc. Any two-digit numeric codes for non-forested site series will be migrated to the alphanumeric codes whenever field guides are updated.

table.name: stocking_layer
table.remarks: Details the standards unit layer information.

column.name: stocking_layer_id
column.remarks: The unique identifier for the STOCKING LAYER

column.name: stocking_standard_unit_id
column.remarks: System generated value uniquely identifing the stocking standards.

column.name: opening_id
column.remarks: System generated value uniquely identifying the opening.

column.name: stocking_layer_code
column.remarks: A code that identifies the forested layer within the Standards Unit to which the preferred/acceptable species information relates.

column.name: target_stocking
column.remarks: The number of well spaced (preferred or acceptable) trees per hectare that will, in normal circumstances, produce an optimal FG crop. Target stocking standards are the standards which silviculture activities are aimed at achieving.

column.name: residual_basal_area
column.remarks: The residual basal area to be left after harvesting in m2/ha. Understood to refer to the mature layer only.

column.name: min_horizontal_distance
column.remarks: Te minimum allowable horizontal distance in metres between trees of the preferred and acceptable species, which is required to be considered to be well-spaced.

column.name: min_pref_stocking_standard
column.remarks: The minimum number of well spaced trees per hectare, of preferred species only, that must be present for the standards unit to be considered SR or FG.

column.name: min_post_spacing
column.remarks: The minimum number of well spaced coniferous trees per hectare to be retained following maximum density spacing.

column.name: min_stocking_standard
column.remarks: The minimum number of well spaced trees per hectare (of preferred and acceptable species) that must be present for the standards unit to be considered SR or FG.

column.name: max_post_spacing
column.remarks: The maximum number of well spaced coniferous trees per hectare to be retained following maximum density spacing.

column.name: max_conifer
column.remarks: The maximum allowable stand density of total countable conifers in order to achieve free growing status.

column.name: hght_relative_to_comp
column.remarks: The height of the tree relative to competing vegetation, within a radius of one metre of the tree trunk, that must be met by a healthy well spaced tree of a preferred or acceptable species at FG.

column.name: tree_size_unit_code
column.remarks: The unit of measure used for height relative to competition. Expressed either in number of centimetres above vegetation (cm), or percentage above vegetation (%).

column.name: entry_userid
column.remarks: The USERID of the individual who entered the information.

column.name: entry_timestamp
column.remarks: The date and time the information was entered.

column.name: update_userid
column.remarks: The USERID of the individual who last updated the information.

column.name: update_timestamp
column.remarks: The date and time of the last update.

column.name: revision_count
column.remarks: A counter identifying the number of times this record as been modified. Used in the web page access to determine if the record as been modified since the data was first retrieved.

table.name: stocking_layer_archive
table.remarks: Contains archived layer information for a Stocking Standard amendment.

column.name: stocking_layer_id
column.remarks: The unique identifier for the STOCKING LAYER

column.name: stocking_event_history_id
column.remarks: Unique identifier for a stocking standard amendment event

column.name: stocking_standard_unit_id
column.remarks: System generated value uniquely identifing the stocking standards.

column.name: opening_id
column.remarks: Opening id of opening this archive record was associated with.

column.name: stocking_layer_code
column.remarks: A code that identifies the forested layer within the Standards Unit to which the preferred/acceptable species information relates.

column.name: min_horizontal_distance
column.remarks: Te minimum allowable horizontal distance in metres between trees of the preferred and acceptable species, which is required to be considered to be well-spaced.

column.name: min_pref_stocking_standard
column.remarks: The minimum number of well spaced trees per hectare, of preferred species only, that must be present for the standards unit to be considered SR or FG.

column.name: min_stocking_standard
column.remarks: The minimum number of well spaced trees per hectare (of preferred and acceptable species) that must be present for the standards unit to be considered SR or FG.

column.name: min_post_spacing
column.remarks: The minimum number of well spaced coniferous trees per hectare to be retained following maximum density spacing.

column.name: residual_basal_area
column.remarks: The residual basal area to be left after harvesting in m2/ha. Understood to refer to the mature layer only.

column.name: target_stocking
column.remarks: The number of well spaced (preferred or acceptable) trees per hectare that will, in normal circumstances, produce an optimal FG crop. Target stocking standards are the standards which silviculture activities are aimed at achieving.

column.name: hght_relative_to_comp
column.remarks: The height of the tree relative to competing vegetation, within a radius of one metre of the tree trunk, that must be met by a healthy well spaced tree of a preferred or acceptable species at FG.

column.name: tree_size_unit_code
column.remarks: The unit of measure used for height relative to competition. Expressed either in number of centimetres above vegetation (cm), or percentage above vegetation (%).

column.name: max_conifer
column.remarks: The maximum allowable stand density of total countable conifers in order to achieve free growing status.

column.name: max_post_spacing
column.remarks: The maximum number of well spaced coniferous trees per hectare to be retained following maximum density spacing.

column.name: entry_userid
column.remarks: The USERID of the individual who entered the information.

column.name: entry_timestamp
column.remarks: The date and time the information was entered.

column.name: update_userid
column.remarks: The USERID of the individual who last updated the information.

column.name: update_timestamp
column.remarks: The date and time of the last update.

column.name: revision_count
column.remarks: A counter identifying the number of times this record as been modified. Used in the web page access to determine if the record as been modified since the data was first retrieved.

column.name: stocking_layer_code
column.remarks: A code that identifies the forested layer within the Standards Unit to which the preferred/acceptable species information relates.

column.name: description
column.remarks: The full description of the code value.

column.name: effective_date
column.remarks: The date the value is available for use.

column.name: expiry_date
column.remarks: The date the value is lo longer available for use.

column.name: update_timestamp
column.remarks: The date and time the value was last modified

table.name: stocking_layer_species
table.remarks: The species of trees and vegetation included in the STOCKING LAYER

column.name: stocking_layer_id
column.remarks: The unique identifier for the STOCKING LAYER

column.name: silv_tree_species_code
column.remarks: The secondary tree species of Crown timber that was cut, damaged, destroyed or removed without authority

column.name: species_order
column.remarks: The order in which the species should be stocked within the STOCKING LAYER.

column.name: preferred_ind
column.remarks: A yes/no indication of whether this is the Preferred or Acceptable Tree Species.

column.name: min_height
column.remarks: The minimum height that a healthy well spaced tree must be, to be considered free growing. Minimum heights vary by species and by site series.

column.name: entry_userid
column.remarks: The USERID of the individual who entered the information.

column.name: entry_timestamp

column.name: update_userid

column.name: update_timestamp

column.name: revision_count
column.remarks: A counter identifying the number of times this record as been modified. Used in the web page access to determine if the record as been modified since the data was first retrieved.

column.remarks: The unique identifier for the STOCKING LAYER

column.name: stocking_event_history_id
column.remarks: Unique identifier for a stocking standard amendment event

column.name: stocking_standard_unit_id

column.name: species_order
column.remarks: The order in which the species should be stocked within the STOCKING LAYER.

column.name: silv_tree_species_code
column.remarks: The secondary tree species of Crown timber that was cut, damaged, destroyed or removed without authority

column.name: preferred_ind
column.remarks: A yes/no indication of whether this is the Preferred or Acceptable Tree Species.

column.name: min_height
column.remarks: The minimum height that a healthy well spaced tree must be, to be considered free growing. Minimum heights vary by species and by site series.

table.name: stocking_milestone
table.remarks: Silviculture milestones that have been achieved for a specific area (SU - Standards Unit). The milestones that are currently tracked are post harvest, regeneration and free growing.

column.name: stocking_standard_unit_id
column.remarks: System generated value uniquely identifing the stocking standards.

column.name: silv_milestone_type_code
column.remarks: A means of identifying if the associated milestone is for post harvest, regeneration or free growing.

column.name: results_submission_id
column.remarks: Used to link information about a data submission. A submission may have many rows of Silviculture (Form A), Activities (Form B), and Forest Cover/Milestones (Form C) information. This attribute will be receiving a system generated number from the Electronic Submission Framework.

column.name: declared_date
column.remarks: The date that the Stocking Standard achieved the Milestone, not necessarily when it was recorded electronically at the Ministry. Previous descriptions from IDD: - The date the post harvest information was declared. - The date on which the Standards Unit was declared regenerated. - An enterable date at which the Standards Unit was declared free growing. This date cannot be in the future. The last date of declaration for all SU"s will become the opening free growing date.

column.name: declared_userid
column.remarks: The userid of the resource who declared the post harvest information.

column.name: declare_ind
column.remarks: A system generated flag "Y/N" which is determined from the polygons which comprise the Standards Unit. If all polygons within the SU are declared free growing then "Y" is displayed. If all SU"s are declared FG then the opening can be declared FG. A Y/N flag indicating whether or not the given Standards unit within the prescription has been declared regenerated.

column.name: declaration_submitted_date
column.remarks: The date that the Milestone declaration was recorded electronically at the Ministry.

column.name: early_offset_years
column.remarks: The number of years, after the start of harvest, when free-growing may first be declared.

column.name: late_offset_years
column.remarks: The number of years, after the start of harvest, by which free-growing must be achieved.

column.name: due_early_date
column.remarks: The earliest year the milestone is to be declared.

column.name: due_late_date
column.remarks: The latest year the milestone is to be declared.

column.name: entry_userid
column.remarks: The USERID of the individual who entered the information.

column.name: entry_timestamp
column.remarks: The date and time the information was entered.

column.name: update_userid
column.remarks: The USERID of the individual who last updated the information.

column.name: update_timestamp
column.remarks: The date and time of the last update.

column.name: revision_count
column.remarks: Internal counter used by the system to avoid conflicting updates to the record.

column.name: extent_feasible_declared_ind
column.remarks: DECLARED TO THE EXTENT PRACTICABLE - Indicator for Milestones that do not meet applicable Stocking Standards.

column.name: stocking_standard_unit_id
column.remarks: System generated value uniquely identifing the stocking standards.

column.name: silv_milestone_type_code
column.remarks: A means of identifying if the associated milestone is for post harvest, regeneration or free growing.

column.name: silviculture_comment_id
column.remarks: System generated value uniquely identifying the ISIS comment.

table.name: stocking_standard_unit
table.remarks: Basic Silviculture objective stated in quantifiable terms for a specific area. These are the acceptable standards for reforestation and soil conversation. Also known as SU - Standards Unit.

column.name: stocking_standard_unit_id
column.remarks: System generated value uniquely identifing the stocking standards.

column.name: opening_id
column.remarks: System generated value uniquely identifying the opening.

column.name: standards_unit_id
column.remarks: A unique identifier within the opening that identifies the standard unit. At least one standards unit per opening is required.

column.name: standards_regime_id
column.remarks: Unique identifier for the STANDARD REGIME entry.

column.name: net_area
column.remarks: A value in hectares for the net area to be reforested (NAR). The NAR is the total area under the prescription (TAUP) minus the area or no planned reforestation (NPR).

column.name: max_allow_soil_disturbance_pct
column.remarks: This is the maximum soil disturbance. The maximum proportion of the net area to be reforested which will be occupied by soil disturbance, following harvest; or following rehab where required.

column.name: variance_ind
column.remarks: A yes/no indicator indicating if this stocking standard is for a variance or not.

column.name: entry_userid
column.remarks: The USERID of the individual who entered the information.

column.name: entry_timestamp
column.remarks: The date and time the information was entered.

column.name: update_userid
column.remarks: The USERID of the individual who last updated the information.

column.name: update_timestamp
column.remarks: The date and time of the last update.

column.name: revision_count
column.remarks: Internal counter used by the system to avoid conflicting updates to the record.

table.name: stocking_standard_unit_amd
table.remarks: Contains Stocking Standard information for an Opening in Amended Status.

column.name: stocking_standard_unit_id
column.remarks: System generated value uniquely identifing the stocking standards.

column.name: opening_id
column.remarks: System generated value uniquely identifying the opening.

column.name: standards_unit_id
column.remarks: A unique identifier within the opening that identifies the standard unit. At least one standards unit per opening is required.

column.name: standards_regime_id
column.remarks: Unique identifier for the STANDARD REGIME entry.

column.name: net_area
column.remarks: A value in hectares for the net area to be reforested (NAR). The NAR is the total area under the prescription (TAUP) minus the area or no planned reforestation (NPR).

column.name: max_allow_soil_disturbance_pct
column.remarks: This is the maximum soil disturbance. The maximum proportion of the net area to be reforested which will be occupied by soil disturbance, following harvest; or following rehab where required.

column.name: variance_ind
column.remarks: A yes/no indicator indicating if this stocking standard is for a variance or not.

column.name: regen_delay_offset_yrs
column.remarks: The number of years allowed for the regeneration of tree growth.

column.name: regen_obligation_ind
column.remarks: A yes/no indicator indicating if this stocking standard is for a regeneration obligation.

column.name: no_regen_early_offset_yrs
column.remarks: The minimum or early regen number of years.

column.name: no_regen_late_offset_yrs
column.remarks: The maximum or late years of regen growth.

column.name: free_growing_early_offset_yrs
column.remarks: The minimum or early growth number of years.

column.name: free_growing_late_offset_yrs
column.remarks: The maximum or late years of tree growth.

column.name: amendment_rationale_comment
column.remarks: A comment describing the rationale for an Amendment to a Stocking Standard.

column.name: entry_userid
column.remarks: The USERID of the individual who entered the information.

column.name: entry_timestamp
column.remarks: The date and time the information was entered.

column.name: update_userid
column.remarks: The USERID of the individual who last updated the information.

column.name: update_timestamp
column.remarks: The date and time of the last update.

column.name: revision_count
column.remarks: Internal counter used by the system to avoid conflicting updates to the record.

table.name: timber_mark
table.remarks: Information about the timber cutting and moving permission for a timber tenure. For Private Timber Marks, the ministry does not grant cutting permission. For some Road Permits and Licences to Cut, no moving permission is granted. Otherwise, synonymous. This entity is not directly updated, but is rather done via a replication process using its base tables, of harvesting authority and hauling authority.

column.name: timber_mark
column.remarks: Unique identifying set of characters to be stamped or marked on the end of each log to associate the log with the specific authority to harvest and move timber.

column.name: forest_file_id
column.remarks: File identification assigned to Provincial Forest Use files. Assigned file number. Usually the Licence, Tenure or Private Mark number.

column.name: cutting_permit_id
column.remarks: Identifier for a cutting permit associated with a quota type harvesting tenure.

column.name: forest_district
column.remarks: Unique physical identifier for the storage of organizational unit codes for the Ministry of Forests offices. Values stored here are for the computers use only, and are not to be used by people as "ministry codes".

column.name: geographic_distrct
column.remarks: Unique physical identifier for the storage of organizational unit codes for the Ministry of Forests offices. Values stored here are for the computers use only, and are not to be used by people as "ministry codes".

column.name: cascade_split_code
column.remarks: Code to indicate the administrative split for the timber mark, east or west of the Cascade Mountains, for tracking timber volume information

column.name: quota_type_code
column.remarks: Identifies an allowable harvesting quota type.

column.name: deciduous_ind
column.remarks: Indicates whether the timber mark is primarily for harvesting deciduous species. Otherwise, coniferous.

column.name: catastrophic_ind
column.remarks: Indicates if the damage to the stand of timber is catastrophic. Otherwise, it is endemic

column.name: crown_granted_ind
column.remarks: Indicator to signify whether the land that the timber mark pertains to has a verifiable crown grant document.

column.name: cruise_based_ind
column.remarks: Indicates whether a cruise was used for billing purposes.

column.name: certificate
column.remarks: Timber mark certificate number for private marks

column.name: hdbs_timber_mark
column.remarks: This is the HDBS representation of the TIMBER MARK. This column is due to be deleted

column.name: vm_timber_mark
column.remarks: This is a reformatted definition of the TIMBER_MARK. It is used primarily in Silviculture Branch"s Major Licence Silviculture Information System. This column is due to be deleted.

column.name: tenure_term
column.remarks: Term of the tenure (in months).

column.name: bcaa_folio_number
column.remarks: BC Assessment Authority Folio Number for the land that the cutting permit/Timber Mark pertains to, if applicable, or the LTO number. BCAA Folio Numbers are in the format C15 733 06604.140-1-2; the format for LTO numbers is nnn-nnn-nnn.

column.name: activated_userid
column.remarks: Userid of the person who activated the timber mark

column.name: amended_userid
column.remarks: Userid of the person who last amended the timber mark.

column.name: district_admn_zone
column.remarks: Identifies an administration area within a district for operational purposes.

column.name: granted_acqrd_date
column.remarks: Date the property that the timber mark pertains to was crown granted; or, in lieu of a crown grant document, the date the land was thought to be acquired; or, a code for an historical land transfer.

column.name: lands_region
column.remarks: Region identifier for crown lands

column.name: crown_granted_acq_desc
column.remarks: The description of the crown granted acquisition.

column.name: mark_status_st
column.remarks: Current status of Timber Mark, eg., Pending - Planned, Harvesting - Suspended. This references Timber_Status_Code.

column.name: mark_status_date
column.remarks: The date that the timber mark / cutting permit status was last updated

column.name: mark_amend_date
column.remarks: If the timber mark has been amended, this date will reflect the time the most recent amendment was made

column.name: mark_appl_date
column.remarks: DATE the application was received in the district.

column.name: mark_cancel_date
column.remarks: Date the timber mark was cancelled.

column.name: mark_extend_date
column.remarks: Date to which the timber mark validity has been extended. Current expiry date

column.name: mark_extend_rsn_cd
column.remarks: Code to indicate the reason to extend the life of the timber mark.

column.name: mark_extend_count
column.remarks: Count of the number of extensions to the expiry date that the timber mark has had.

column.name: mark_issue_date
column.remarks: Date the Timber Mark was issued

column.name: mark_expiry_date
column.remarks: Initial expiry date set upon activation of the timber mark.

column.name: markng_instrmnt_cd
column.remarks: Code indicating the means of marking the timber for identification to the timber mark, eg., hammer, paint.

column.name: marking_method_cd
column.remarks: Code indicating the method of marking the truckload of harvested timber

column.name: entry_userid
column.remarks: The userid of the individual performing the entry.

column.name: entry_timestamp
column.remarks: The data and time on which the entry was created.

column.name: update_userid
column.remarks: The userid of the individual who last updated this information

column.name: update_timestamp
column.remarks: The date and time of the last update

column.name: revision_count
column.remarks: A count of the number of times an entry in the entity has been modified. Used to validate if the current information displayed on a user"s web browser is the most current.

column.name: small_patch_salvage_ind
column.remarks: Indicates whether or not Cutting Permit is for small patch salvage. Geometry does not have to be supplied for small patch salvage cut blocks that are under 1 hectare.

column.name: salvage_type_code
column.remarks: Code describing the salvage type.

column.name: tree_cover_pattern_code
column.remarks: The dispursion of trees over the area.

column.name: description
column.remarks: The full description of the code value.

column.name: effective_date
column.remarks: The date the value is available for use.

column.name: expiry_date
column.remarks: The date the value is lo longer available for use.

column.name: update_timestamp
column.remarks: The date and time the value was last modified

column.remarks: The unit of measure used for height relative to competition. Expressed either in number of centimetres above vegetation (cm), or percentage above vegetation (%).

column.name: expiry_date
column.remarks: The date the value is no longer available for use.

column.name: update_timestamp
column.remarks: The date and time the value was last modified

column.remarks: The secondary tree species of Crown timber that was cut, damaged, destroyed or removed without authority

column.name: expiry_date

column.remarks: The unique code identifying a Timber Supply Block.

column.name: expiry_date
column.remarks: The date after which the value is no longer available for use.

column.name: update_timestamp

column.remarks: A code to denote the unit basis on which work was tendered (e.g. days, hectares). Subset of UNIT OF MEASURE CODE.

routine.name: pgp_armor_headers(text, OUT key text, OUT value text)
